message:
Welcome to Too Good To Be True, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
*Incident Number INC100013:* P2 test
Welcome our new IM @PLAYER, Happy to have you in the team. Hope you get comfortable with everyone
Welcome @PLAYER
Nice to meet you @PLAYER
There are a few strange customer reports with the helpdesk
Apparently, the site is loading slowly
People are reporting that they can't make payments either
@PLAYER Can you check this?
Sure, I can start to take a look with the team
@bob Can you give me some information on what users are reporting? What's the issue with the site?
Let me gather the information for you
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
There are more than 300 customer reports till now
Calls are from every region, likely to be Global
There's a bunch of messages coming in. A few people saying they can't do anything on the site
Let me know if you need more information
I see a lot of noise in Social media- do we have an issue ? @matt
Okay, before we confirm regions and where the impact is I can confirm that our observability tooling is showing strong signs that we do have some issue with the platform
:star: Qualify the problem
Yes, looks like no users are able to login
@tanya @hamed Can you confirm that you're also seeing issues in our observability metrics? I've looked at Grafana
Sure @Matt - Checking
:star: Facts over opinion
I am away from desk atm
I definitely see issues in front endpods
Can we bring in app teams as well please
Can I get some guidance from the team for how severe we think this issue is? We'll need to report it to the wider business and open up an incident
:bulb: Understand the problem by reproducing it
@bez I can confirm that we have an issue, I'll open an incident report now and confirm details with you soon. I'm working with others in the tech team now
Get this sorted asap please ,Do we have Team A to resolve this
@tanya @daniel @shay Are we in agreement that this is classified as P1 severity? I can confirm that I cannot access the website at all, seems pretty serious
Yes, i need incident to login to servers, do wehave INC # please
:star: Reproduces the incident
:star: Take command
Yes, the whole system is down - getting 502
*Incident Number INC100086:* P1 Website is down
:star: Incident record created
:star: Send first comms in first 5 minutes
How many customers are affected? Are the reports coming in from all regions?
@bob Can you confirm with Bez the number of reports and regions we're seeing issues in please
Sure, its global issue and there are more than 400+ customer reports
@daniel incident is open, number is INC100086
Looking into this and trying to replicate the issue
I am able to replicate the issue. Checking the logs now
I see a lot of errors, most of them are context initialisation errors
Yes replicated the issue and even i'm seeing similar errors in frontend logs
I have a stake holders meet in next 10 minutes, @PLAYER do you think this can be resolved by then?
@bez Sure, its global issue and there are more than 400+ customer reports, confirmed by customer facing team
Thanks, keep me posted
WIll do, I have the team replicated the issue and looking into the logs now. Hopefully we can identify the issue and get a resolution in before your meeting
thanks
:star: Send regular business comms
Looks like the front end has crashed or failed to start up. The other logs suggest some missing configuration, do you agree @daniel ?
Most likely yes
:bulb: Check the changes done in the platform
Can someone from the engineering team give some insight into what might have changed recently? Do we have any new features, released that would require this config?
Below are the changes implemented this morning
:star: Check changes done to the platform
If you want more information, checkout the change details : https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/ZmRkZTQ1ODg2M2U1NGM3NDk1ZjZhM2I3MWY0MTM5ODA
It looks like it's an issue with something we released earlier today
:star: Reviewed changes
Due to change !! again - oh
Confirming with the team now
Okay only two changes to PROD, could be one of those
yes
@shay and @daniel can you look into your changes? See if there's anything that looks like it could have an impact on environment variables or local config?
I have verified Payments change (CHG89347), there are no errors and the service is up and healthy
Checkout service change(CHG89350) is in UAT, so not affecting PROD. But I see a few errors from Frontend pods
All the errors started right after the change (CHG89349) on the Frontend
A Frontend change caused by change CHG89349 has brought down the Frontend pods
The customer complaints are rising. @PLAYER Do we have an ETA for resolution?
What is the latest on this?
But this functionally worked when I tested
Even if it worked in your test environment, something's behaving differently in prod, maybe a config mismatch or unhandled edge case
Can you give a quick update on the status?
I think I agree with Daniel, the errors are coming from the Front End Service and your changes are related to that. They are destructive changes as well
We might be able to fix forward, but would it be best for us to revert these changes and go back to last known stable?
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Its best we revert asap
Okay, how long will that take?
:star: Manage actions and timings
Sorry, I've never done a rollback in PROD before, can someone help me with the steps?
Of course! Who can help Shay out?
I will help you, @Shay. Rolling back now
I have rollbacked Frontend:version to 10.1.0 to more stable version. Can somebody verify?

To verify resolution and close incident:
--Validate fix with team:white_check_mark:
--Check with Customer Support:white_check_mark:
--Confirm in 'Business-comms':white_check_mark:
We think we've found the change that caused the issue, we're going to rollback now to a healthy state
Customers are able to place the orders, complaint calls have reduced as well.
will confirm once that's done
I can verify it
Can confirm that grafana metrics look healthy again, lots of 200s and user activity
:star: Use metrics as evidence
:star: Validate the fix
Roll back complete, we can confirm that the site is operational again
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Thats good,
are we back up ?
Yes we are
I can confirm that I can place orders again and customers have confirmed this
Thanks , appreciate quick turn around
I can access the site again and place orders
Y^es,
Front end is give 200's now
can i let customers know we are back ?
Let's schedule in a retrospective to talk about the changes we shipped
@bob you can confirm with customers that we should be back online
It's definitely working again!
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Wow that was intense!
Thanks! for resolving it quickly
Good job guys
Yes well done! that could have gone much worse so props to everyone
We will have a Post-Incident Review (PIR) to understand what happened
Sure, i ll do restrospectve

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.
You're welcome! Let us know if you need any more information

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/29/7613/feedback
